result.summary: Dropbox exposes a documented HTTP API for user and business/team operations, two OAuth-protected remote MCP servers, and Dropbox Drop-ins web components (Chooser/Saver/Embedder) initialized with an app key.
result.description: Dropbox is a cloud storage and collaboration service for storing, sharing, and managing files and folders. It also offers business administration features through the Dropbox Business platform and AI-connected access through Dropbox MCP endpoints.

dropbox_oauth_app.label: Dropbox OAuth app (app key/secret) and generated access tokens
dropbox_oauth_app.setup: Create an app in the [Dropbox App Console](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps). In the app settings, choose permissions/scopes, set redirect URIs, and note the app key and app secret. For quick testing on your own account, you can use the **Generate** button in the OAuth 2 section to mint an access token. For production integrations, send users through Dropbox OAuth and exchange the code at `https://api.dropboxapi.com/oauth2/token`. OAuth app registration is documented in the [OAuth Guide](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/reference/oauth-guide) and [Getting Started](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/reference/getting-started).

dropbox_dropin_app_key.label: Dropbox app key for Drop-ins components (Chooser, Saver, Embedder)
dropbox_dropin_app_key.setup: Create an app at [Create app](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps/create) or in the [App Console](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps). For web integrations, add your site domains (or `localhost`) in the app settings and then place the returned app key into the `data-app-key` attribute of the `https://www.dropbox.com/static/api/2/dropins.js` script tag. Dropbox documents this setup on the [Chooser](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/chooser), [Saver](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/saver), and [Embedder](https://www.dropbox.com/developers/embedder) pages.
